mnemonics.title: JOIN Types Memory
mnemonics.content: L-R-F: Left-Right-Full - Outer joins go in alphabetical order (Left first, then Right, then Full)

mnemonics.title: Theta Join Steps
mnemonics.content: θ-Join = Cross then Select (Cartesian Product then Selection with θ condition)

mnemonics.title: Division Meaning
mnemonics.content: R ÷ S means 'Find X values that have ALL Y values associated' - think of it as 'FOR ALL' query operator

mnemonics.title: Natural Join vs Outer Join
mnemonics.content: Natural Join = Inner (matches only), Outer Join = Include Outside (non-matching too)

mnemonics.title: Semijoin Purpose
mnemonics.content: Semijoin = Semi (half) - returns half the info, only attributes from one relation

acronyms.expansion: Theta Join - general join condition (can be =, <, >, ≤, ≥, ≠)
acronyms.usage: Used for complex join conditions beyond equality

acronyms.expansion: Natural Join - automatic equality on common attribute names
acronyms.usage: Simplifies equijoin when relations share attribute names

acronyms.expansion: Left/Right/Full Outer Join - preserves unmatched tuples

acronyms.usage: Finding entities associated with ALL values of another set

visualTips.visualization: Draw two circles (relations) with overlapping section (common attributes). Natural join = only the overlap, but with all attributes combined.
Venn diagram where Left OJ = entire left circle + overlap, Right OJ = entire right circle + overlap, Full = both circles completely filled.
Think of division as: Given S (requirements), find which X values satisfy ALL requirements in S. Like 'Find students who passed ALL tests'.
Arrow flow: R → [× Cartesian] → S → [σ Select] θ → Result. Two-step process shown as pipeline.

analogies.analogy: Like matching students to their ID cards - only pairs where student ID matches ID card number, one combined record per match.
Like a class photo with all students - even students without partners get included (shown as placeholder).
Like getting a list of students who have books, but only student names (not the book details).
Finding which teachers teach ALL subjects - you check each teacher against complete subject list.
Like a dating app filter - you specify criteria (age > 25, income > 50k), then match pairs meeting those conditions.
